response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software craftsman with strong web, mobile, and some embedded/low-level C/C++ and DLang experience, but the role is a hardware-focused Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerators. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design at the silicon level — the core requirements of this position. The performance work shown is application-level (low-latency services, high-throughput APIs), not chip/accelerator architecture.
strengths: Deep, long-tenured software engineering background across many domains
Some low-level C/C++ and embedded exposure (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
Demonstrated work on low-latency, high-throughput performance-sensitive systems
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, deep-learning workload, or GPU architecture background
Lacks SIMD/MIMD and hardware-software co-design qualifications; BS only, no advanced research depth in the domain

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator / hardware architecture experience
Missing SoC/IP modeling and DNN optimization skills
